vol.label: Spillover — VIX turns higher for the first time this episode
decomp: VIX +3.02% to 17.08 — first up day in the vol complex since the Korea rout began
SOXX -6.96%, MU -7.32%, broad US semis now selling off hard, not just Korea
EWY -5.71% to $174.90 — the earlier flat/decoupled read has broken down
MOVE still flat — bond vol not yet confirming the equity move

bias: This run marks a real inflection. EWY, which had been eerily flat while KOSPI cratered, finally broke down hard (-5.71% to $174.90, widening its gap below the 196.80 SMA20 even further). More importantly, the 'contained, Korea-only' thesis from a few hours ago has broken: US-listed semis and enabling-layer names are now selling off broadly in NY afternoon trading — SOXX -6.96%, MU -7.32%, DRAM -10.26%, GLW -11.87%, LITE -10.86%, AMKR -12.64%, POWI -9.62%, CRDO -8.68%, EOSE -6.94% — and VIX has flipped from falling to rising (+3.02%), its first red print since this episode began. Financials cracked too: ALLY -2.80%, CACC -1.01%, COF -0.79%, the first negative day for lenders after three sessions of calm. Gold is still elevated (+1.91%) though off its intraday peak, and USDKRW continued easing toward 1,530 (now 1,538, the closest approach yet), but Korea's own market (KOSPI, SK Hynix, Samsung) is closed for the day and unchanged from this morning's print. Read together: what looked like an idiosyncratic Korean event through midday now looks like it is spilling into broader US risk sentiment. Korea gate remains firmly 0/3. Treat this as a genuine regime-confidence downgrade, not just a data update — the divergence that anchored the 'contained' call is gone.

counterargs.thesis: The Korea/semis shock has broadened into genuine US risk-off, not stayed contained
supporting: VIX +3.02% — first up day in the vol complex since the episode began
SOXX -6.96%, MU -7.32%, broad US-listed semis reversing hard after this morning's brief stabilization
ALLY/CACC/COF all red — first crack in financials after three calm sessions
EWY itself finally broke down -5.71%, ending the earlier flat/decoupled pattern
against: MOVE still flat — bond-market vol shows zero confirmation of the equity move
HY OAS unchanged at 2.74% — credit spreads themselves aren't widening, only equity-side lender names
Gold pulled back from its earlier +2.46% peak to +1.91% — safe-haven bid isn't accelerating
KOSPI/SK Hynix/Samsung unchanged today (Korea market closed) — no fresh Korea-specific catalyst confirmed
counterargs.read: The clean Korea-vs-US decoupling from earlier today did not hold through the US session. Whether this is a genuine regime shift or a single rough US afternoon reversing on a lighter catalyst (index rebalancing, momentum unwind, options-related flows) isn't yet clear — MOVE and HY OAS staying flat argue for the latter. But VIX turning positive and financials cracking are exactly the kind of broadening signals that would justify treating this as more than noise. Watch tomorrow's Asia open and whether US vol holds these levels overnight.

trade_table.rationale: This morning's US-listed stabilization (MU +1.1%, SOXX flat) fully reversed — SOXX -6.96%, MU -7.32%, AMKR -12.64%. No longer a Korea-only story.
Curve drifted modestly higher (10Y 4.485%, +1bp vs midday). SOFR steady near IORB. Still awaiting the delayed H.4.1 print.
Gate still 0/3. EWY itself finally cracked -5.71% to $174.90, now well below its 196.80 SMA20. KOSPI unchanged at 7,648.10 (market closed).
ALLY -2.80%, CACC -1.01%, COF -0.79% — first red day after three sessions of strength. HY OAS itself still tight at 2.74%, watch for confirmation.
DXY -0.53% on the day, USDJPY still below 162. Dollar weakness continuing to hold.
HYG roughly flat, OAS steady at 2.74%. Credit itself not yet confirming the equity-vol spillover.
Jul–Sep reserve drain still ahead. Today's broader risk-off adds a near-term reason for caution on top of the structural case.

catalysts.note: TGA + Reserves print still not posted as of 19:58 CET — running late. Watch for a possible slip to tomorrow.
Strong print = Warsh hike back on table. Weak print = duration relief. Lands right into a now-jumpier vol backdrop.
Bid-to-cover and tail will show whether private demand absorbs supply at these higher yields.
PCE peak narrative confirmed or reversed. Hot CPI = duration selloff + Warsh hike.
APH $165/$170 spread now trading OUT of the money (APH $162.51) — reassess. DRAM $69/$74 and EOSE $7.50/$10 both deep OTM after today's moves.
Quarterly Refunding Announcement. Auction sizes + TGA guidance for Q3.

Accelerating lower again, now over 13% off the $274 ATH.

50 shares @ $38.57 — this morning's +3.35% bounce fully reversed; still above cost basis but the cushion is narrowing fast.
Sharpest drop of the episode, off the $96 ATH by ~27%. Watchlist only, no active position.
$165/$170 Jul 17 spread now trading OUT of the money (APH below both strikes) — reassess this position.
25 shares @ $84.21 — now down over 13% from cost basis, worst reading yet.
$7.50/$10 Jul 17 spread (10 contracts). Thesis broken, continuing lower. CLOSE PRIORITY.
